codeReview.summary: Foundation scaffolding is substantial and core pieces exist (MCP hub, proxy, DB schema, daemon lifecycle), but there are milestone-contract mismatches that block acceptance for f1.

issues.description: `service status` does not report uptime, active integrations count, or connected agents count required by A-CLI-02; it only prints running state, PID, and URLs.

Initialize requests without explicit `Accept: application/json, text/event-stream` are rejected with `Not Acceptable`, while the feature verification step and expected behavior use curl without this header.

No runtime path exists to register downstream MCP connections in the daemon process (no config load/CLI/API wiring), so A-MCP-03/A-MCP-04 cannot be demonstrated through the product surface despite proxy internals existing.
CLI status output is missing contract-required fields (uptime, active integrations count, connected agents count).
issues.evidence: src/cli/index.ts:69-73 prints only running/PID/URLs; src/daemon/manager.ts:104-119 returns no uptime or counts.
issues.suggestedFix: Extend DaemonManager.status() and CLI status rendering to include uptime, active integrations, and connected agents from runtime state.

issues.description: Feature verification command for MCP initialize is incompatible with current server behavior because required Accept header is omitted.
issues.evidence: features.json:182 verification step omits Accept header; targeted check returned `{"error":{"message":"Not Acceptable: Client must accept both application/json and text/event-stream"}}` when using that exact command.
issues.suggestedFix: Either relax server content negotiation to accept default curl Accept behavior, or update mission validation/verification commands to include `Accept: application/json, text/event-stream` consistently.

issues.description: Downstream MCP aggregation is implemented at class level but not exposed via foundation runtime flow, leaving A-MCP-03/A-MCP-04 unverifiable from daemon+CLI surfaces.
issues.evidence: src/server/mcp-hub.ts:277 has addProxy(), but src/main.ts has no loading/registration path and src/cli/index.ts has only start/stop/status commands.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a minimal foundation registration path (seed config or CLI/API hookup) so at least one downstream MCP can be connected and called end-to-end.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature uses `skillName: worker`, but `.factory/skills` is empty in this repo and transcript shows worker-skill invocation failed.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: This caused procedure deviation (`followedProcedure: false`) and repeated ambiguity; add or document canonical worker skill location to prevent recurring drift.

sharedStateObservations.observation: mcp-hub health check is `curl ... http://localhost:3333/mcp` without MCP headers/body.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: `/mcp` expects proper MCP transport semantics and can return non-200 for naive checks; use `/health`-style endpoint or a valid initialize probe for reliable service health monitoring.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No architecture reference file exists at `.factory/library/architecture.md` for reviewer boundary checks.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Review workflow expects architecture guidance; adding/maintaining it would reduce reviewer uncertainty about intended component boundaries.

commandsRun.observation: Transcript skeleton located and reviewed for the assigned worker session.
Reviewed commit scope and full diff for f1 implementation commit.
CLI exposes only start/stop/status commands in current runtime.

commandsRun.command: cd /Users/teodorwaltervido/Desktop/service && node dist/cli/index.js start && sleep 2 && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3333/mcp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' && node dist/cli/index.js stop
commandsRun.observation: Returned JSON-RPC error `Not Acceptable` (content negotiation failure) for the feature's documented initialize command.

commandsRun.command: cd /Users/teodorwaltervido/Desktop/service && node dist/cli/index.js start && sleep 2 && curl -i -s -X POST http://localhost:3333/mcp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"review","version":"1.0"}}}' && node dist/cli/index.js stop
commandsRun.observation: Initialize succeeds with explicit Accept header and returns MCP session + valid initialize payload.

summary: Verdict: fail. Core foundation code exists, but there are blocking contract mismatches: incomplete CLI status fields, initialize verification/header incompatibility, and no end-to-end downstream MCP registration path exposed from runtime surfaces.
